summary: Iteration 15 — Avatar step D-pad hardening + sticky preview header. 8 of 9 review requirements verified PASS on live preview URL. ONE FAILURE: ArrowRight at the right edge of avatar-row-custom is a NO-OP instead of wrapping to the next row (test req: 'wrap to the next row if exhausted'). Tests confirmed: sticky preview renders with inner [data-testid^=avatar-] and 'Pick your avatar' heading; sticky stays pinned after initial scroll-settlement; ArrowDown reliably walks 5 distinct rows (custom→animals→wild→fantasy→sports→music); ArrowUp returns focus to avatar-row-custom; live preview updates with focused id (avatar-build-new → avatar-a51 after 2× Down); focused tile stays on-screen (top=472 / bottom=601 / winH=1080); 0 console errors across 10 mixed arrow presses; direct click on avatar-pick-a3 opens save-avatar-confirm with avatar-a3 preview.

ui_bugs.issue: ArrowRight (and by symmetry ArrowLeft) is a NO-OP at the horizontal end of a row instead of wrapping to the next row. With localStorage 'onnowtv-custom-avatars-v1' empty, [data-testid=avatar-build-your-own] is the only focusable in avatar-row-custom; pressing ArrowRight 5× kept focus on 'build-new' all 5 times. Review requirement: 'Each press should land on a distinct subsequent focusable in the same row (or wrap to the next row if exhausted). NO press should be a no-op until a row boundary.'

tests_executed.test1_sticky_header_and_inner_circle: PASS — At /profiles/new step 2: [data-testid=avatar-sticky-preview] count=1, inner [data-testid^=avatar-] = 'avatar-a1' on entry (initial avatarId), heading 'Pick your avatar' count=1.
tests_executed.test2_sticky_stays_pinned: PASS (with nuance) — Sticky preview top across 6× ArrowDown: [227.7, 144.7, 74, 74, 74, 74, 74]. After the page scroll catches up on the first 2 presses, the sticky element is pinned at top=74 and stays there steady for the remaining 4+ presses (delta=0px). The initial 227→74 movement is the page scrolling its sticky parent into view, not the sticky element drifting; this is standard CSS sticky behaviour. Once pinned, it does NOT move as further rows slide underneath.
tests_executed.test3_arrowright_reliability: FAIL — From [data-testid=avatar-build-your-own] focus, 5× ArrowRight returned ['build-new','build-new','build-new','build-new','build-new','build-new']. The scoped handler computes target=null when colIdx+1>=rows[rowIdx].length and returns early (no wrap-to-next-row). With an empty customAvatars list, the Build tile is the only data-focusable in avatar-row-custom so every right press is a no-op. Re-tested with one pre-seeded custom avatar (id='custom-prefilled') in localStorage 'onnowtv-custom-avatars-v1' — same result: ['build-new', 'build-new', 'build-new', 'build-new']. Likely the customAvatars useMemo with [] deps captures pre-mount state OR the prefilled tile isn't being registered as focusable in time; either way the requirement is not satisfied.
tests_executed.test4_arrowdown_reliability: PASS — From avatar-build-your-own focus, 5× ArrowDown landed in 5 DIFFERENT rows in order: avatar-row-animals(a1) → avatar-row-wild(a51) → avatar-row-fantasy(a16) → avatar-row-sports(a26) → avatar-row-music(a34). All 5 are distinct and none is avatar-row-custom.
tests_executed.test5_arrowup_reliability: PASS — After 5× ArrowDown (focus on avatar-row-music a34), 5× ArrowUp returned focus to avatar-row-custom with id='build-new'. Travel back up works without losing focus.
tests_executed.test6_live_preview_updates: PASS — With avatar-build-your-own focused: sticky preview inner = 'avatar-build-new'. After 2× ArrowDown (focus moved to avatar-row-wild tile a51): sticky preview inner = 'avatar-a51' — matches focused data-avatar-id exactly.
tests_executed.test7_scrollintoview_visibility: PASS — After 6× ArrowDown: focused tile bounding rect top=472.2, bottom=601.8, window.innerHeight=1080. Tile is comfortably below sticky preview (>100) AND above page bottom (<980).
tests_executed.test8_zero_console_errors: PASS — 0 console.error events across full sequence: step-1 type 'test' → Next → step-2 → 10 mixed arrow presses (Down, Right, Down, Left, Up, Right, Down, Down, Up, Left) → click avatar-pick-a3 → save-avatar-confirm. console_errors=[].
tests_executed.test9_click_routes_through_save_confirm: PASS — Direct Playwright click on [data-testid=avatar-pick-a3] opened [data-testid=save-avatar-confirm] modal within 1s. Modal preview inner [data-testid^=avatar-] = 'avatar-a3' (panda) — matches the tile that was clicked. Headline 'Save this as your icon?' visible with No / Yes,save buttons.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): In ProfileEdit.jsx AvatarStep keydown handler (~line 1379-1404), when ArrowRight overflows the current row (colIdx+1 >= rows[rowIdx].length), wrap to the next row's first tile. Symmetrically for ArrowLeft → previous row's last tile. Currently target stays null and the handler returns without preventDefault, leaving the user stuck (review requirement explicitly: 'or wrap to the next row if exhausted').

VERIFY: When a pre-seeded custom avatar exists in onnowtv-custom-avatars-v1, the row should expose it as data-focusable. In this iter's test the prefilled tile didn't appear to register — ArrowRight still no-op'd. Could be a useMemo([]) freshness issue (loadCustomAvatars runs at mount, before localStorage hydrate?) or the tile renders without [data-focusable='true']. Inspect customAvatars row render path.
critical_code_review_comments: ProfileEdit.jsx is now 2633 lines (up from 2415 in iter 14 → 2633 in iter 15, +218 lines for the scoped handler + sticky header). File-split refactor is overdue: extract AvatarStep, AvatarRow, BuildAvatarOverlay, ChipRow into /components/avatar/*.

The scoped capture-phase keydown handler in AvatarStep uses useEffect with [] deps so getRows() is rebuilt every keystroke (acceptable for ~150 buttons, but for-loop searching rows[r].indexOf(active) every press is O(rows*tiles) per key). Cache row index on focus event for cleaner perf.
Edge case (current bug): no horizontal-wrap-to-next-row on ArrowRight/Left at row edges. Either implement wrap, or accept the no-op behaviour but then update the testable requirement in the prompt.

Sticky preview top: -6 with backdropFilter:'blur(12px)' + linear-gradient is layered nicely, but the negative -6 puts the top 6px of the preview off the visible top of the scroll container — minor aesthetic, may hide the focus ring's top stroke on the AvatarCircle when pinned.

useSpatialFocus is declared locked but the scoped handler uses window.addEventListener with capture=true and e.stopPropagation — confirmed working: 10 mixed arrow presses produced 0 errors and the global engine never interfered. Good defence-in-depth.

loadCustomAvatars() inside React.useMemo(()=>..., []) is fine but means a custom avatar saved THEN navigating back to step 2 in the SAME mount won't show — only after re-mount. Currently OK because save-confirm triggers an advance to step 3, but worth a comment.

success_rate.frontend: 89% (8 of 9 requirements PASS; ArrowRight wrap-to-next-row missing)

seed_data_creation: Used Playwright addInitScript: localStorage 'onnowtv-profiles-v1'=[{id:'test-iter15',name:'TestIter15',kids:false}], 'onnowtv-active-profile-v1'='test-iter15', cleared 'onnowtv-custom-avatars-v1'. Secondary test ran localStorage.setItem of onnowtv-custom-avatars-v1 with one prefilled custom record then reload — same ArrowRight no-op result. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 15 ships sticky preview header [data-testid=avatar-sticky-preview] with inner AvatarCircle reading the FOCUSED avatar (state focusedId, mirrored via focusin listener), plus a scoped capture-phase window keydown handler on AvatarStep that walks data-avatar-row→data-focusable tiles. Sticky behaviour, ArrowDown/Up navigation, live preview update on focus change, scroll-into-view visibility, zero console errors, and click→save-avatar-confirm all verified PASS. SINGLE FAILURE: ArrowRight (and presumably ArrowLeft) is no-op at row horizontal edges — does NOT wrap to next row as the review prompt requires. Test selectors confirmed working: [data-testid=avatar-sticky-preview], [data-testid=avatar-build-your-own] (data-avatar-id='build-new'), [data-testid=avatar-row-custom/animals/wild/fantasy/sports/music/faces/...], [data-testid=avatar-pick-a<n>] (data-avatar-id=a<n>), [data-testid=save-avatar-confirm]. On-screen keyboard letters lowercase (page.get_by_role('button', name='t', exact=True)); ENTER button label is 'ENTER' BUT clicking the bottom CTA 'Next: choose an avatar' is more reliable. After scrolling, sticky preview pins at top=74 in 1920×1080 viewport. avatar-row-faces appears as a 6th row when navigating further (so the AVATAR_CATEGORIES list is animals/wild/fantasy/sports/music/faces/...).
rca_of_the_issue: Bug: ArrowRight at horizontal end of row is a no-op. RCA: In /app/frontend/src/pages/ProfileEdit.jsx around line 1379-1386, the handler does `if (colIdx + 1 < rows[rowIdx].length) target = rows[rowIdx][colIdx + 1];` and likewise for Left. When the condition fails, target stays null and the handler returns at line 1405 (`if (!target) return;`) without preventDefault — leaving focus stuck. Reproduction: clear localStorage 'onnowtv-custom-avatars-v1', navigate to /profiles/new step 2, focus [data-testid=avatar-build-your-own], press ArrowRight — focus stays on build-new (verified 5× consecutively, 0 movement). Mitigation: when colIdx is at the row's last index AND ArrowRight, set target = rows[rowIdx+1]?.[0] (if next row exists); symmetric for ArrowLeft with rows[rowIdx-1]?.[length-1].
